lessons.title: The Arithmetic and Logic Unit; ALU, Control Unit and Registers (Program Counter; PC, Accumulator; ACC, Memory Address Register; MAR, Memory Data Register; MDR, Current Instruction Register; CIR). Buses: data, address and control: how this relates to assembly language programs.

lessons.title: (a) Primitive data types, integer, real/floating point, character, string and Boolean. (b) Represent positive integers in binary. (c) Use of sign and magnitude and two’s complement to represent negative numbers in binary. (d) Addition and subtraction of binary integers. (e) Represent positive integers in hexadecimal. (f) Convert positive integers between binary hexadecimal and denary. (g) Positive and negative real numbers using normalised floating point representation. (h) How character sets (ASCII and UNICODE) are used to represent text.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: (a) Arrays (of up to 3 dimensions), records, lists, tuples.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: (b)The properties of stacks and queues.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Define problems using Boolean logic
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: (b) Manipulate Boolean expressions, including the use of Karnaugh maps to simplify Boolean expressions.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: (c) Use logic gate diagrams and truth tables.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: (a) The Data Protection Act 1998. (b) The Computer Misuse Act 1990. (c) The Copyright Design and Patents Act 1988. (d) The Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Thinking abstractly (a) The nature of abstraction. (b) The need for abstraction. (c) The differences between an abstraction and reality. (d) Devise an abstract model for a variety of situations.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Thinking ahead (a) Identify the inputs and outputs for a given situation. (b) Determine the preconditionsThinking ahead for devising a solution to a problem. (c) The need for reusable program components.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Thinking procedurally (a) Identify the components of a problem. (b) Identify the components of a solution to a problem. (c) Determine the order of the steps needed to solve a problem. (d) Identify sub-procedures necessary to solve a problem.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Thinking logically (a) Identify the points in a solution where a decision has to be taken. (b) Determine the logical conditions that affect the outcome of a decision. (c) Determine how decisions affect flow through a program.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Programming techniques (a) Programming constructs: sequence, iteration branching. (b) Global and local variables. (c) Modularity, functions and procedures, parameter passing by value and reference. (d) Use of an IDE to develop/debug a program.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: Software Development (a) Understand the waterfall lifecycle, agile methodologies, extreme programming, the spiral model and rapid application development. b ) The relative merits and drawbacks of different methodologies and when they might be used. (c) Writing and following algorithms. (d) Different test strategies, including black and white box testing and alpha and beta testing. (e) Test programs that solve problems using suitable test data and end user feedback, justify a test strategy for a given situation.
lessons.programme: OCR AS/A Level Computer Science H046/H446

lessons.title: (a) Analysis and design of algorithms for a given situation.(d) Implement binary and linear search.

lessons.title: (f) Compare the suitability of different algorithms for a given task and data set.
